title: Flight Test Card Package
description: Plan a small flight-test campaign by writing test cards, defining build-up logic, and specifying what data must be captured at each point.
whyItMatters: Flight test is where predictions meet reality. Good test cards protect people, protect hardware, and make sure each flight produces usable evidence instead of vague impressions.

steps.title: Choose the campaign objective
steps.description: Select one clear goal such as stall-characterisation, climb performance, or handling-qualities assessment.
steps.tips: One campaign should answer a focused question, not every question.

steps.title: Write the build-up sequence
steps.description: Define how you will move from low-risk to higher-risk points and what conditions must be satisfied before the next step.
steps.tips: Include specific abort triggers rather than general caution statements.

steps.title: Draft individual test cards
steps.description: For each flight point, specify conditions, pilot actions, required instrumentation, and expected data outputs.
steps.tips: The card should be usable in the cockpit without interpretation.

steps.title: Plan the safety and telemetry loop
steps.description: Describe who is monitoring the test, what data is watched live, and how go or no-go decisions are made.
steps.tips: Flight-test discipline is as much about communication as analysis.

steps.title: Review against the objective
steps.description: Check that the full package would actually generate enough evidence to support the decision you care about.
steps.tips: Test points without a downstream decision are usually waste.

deliverables: Three to five complete test cards

rubric.description: The package reflects a credible incremental approach with explicit stop criteria.
Each test point produces data that supports the stated objective.
Pilots and engineers could execute the cards without ambiguity.
Instrumentation and data capture are aligned to the decision being supported.
